steps.title: Use the isosceles triangle
steps.content: In triangle $ACD$, $AC=CD$, so the base angles are equal. Since angle $ACD$ is $108^\circ$, $CAD=CDA=\frac{180^\circ-108^\circ}{2}=36^\circ$.

steps.title: Find the exterior angle
steps.content: From the figure, angle $BCE$ measures $60^\circ$. In triangle $BCE$, the exterior angle at $E$ is the sum of the two remote interior angles: $BED=42^\circ+60^\circ=102^\circ$.

steps.title: Use the straight line
steps.content: Angle $BED$ and $x$ form a straight line, so they are supplementary. Therefore $x=180^\circ-102^\circ=78^\circ$. The answer is 78.
